deva:
कर्मण्येवाधिकारस्ते मा फलेषु कदाचन।
मा कर्मफलहेतुर्भूर्मा ते सङ्गोऽस्त्वकर्मणि॥
iast: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana | mā karma-phala-hetur bhūr mā te saṅgo 'stv akarmaṇi
en: Your right is to the work alone, never to its fruits. Let not the fruit be your motive, nor let your love be for inaction.

deva:
न जायते म्रियते वा कदाचि-
न्नायं भूत्वा भविता वा न भूयः।
अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो
न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥
iast: na jāyate mriyate vā kadācin nāyaṁ bhūtvā bhavitā vā na bhūyaḥ | ajo nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṁ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre
en: It is never born, and it never dies. Unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient, it is not slain when the body is slain.

deva:
यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत।
अभ्युत्थानमधर्मस्य तदात्मानं सृजाम्यहम्॥
परित्राणाय साधूनां विनाशाय च दुष्कृताम्।
धर्मसंस्थापनार्थाय सम्भवामि युगे युगे॥
iast: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata … sambhavāmi yuge yuge
en: Whenever dharma withers and what is against it rises, O Bharata, I send myself forth. To shelter the good, to unmake the wicked, to set dharma firm again, I take birth, age after age.

deva:
दिवि सूर्यसहस्रस्य भवेद्युगपदुत्थिता।
यदि भाः सदृशी सा स्याद्भासस्तस्य महात्मनः॥
iast: divi sūrya-sahasrasya bhaved yugapad utthitā | yadi bhāḥ sadṛśī sā syād bhāsas tasya mahātmanaḥ
en: If a thousand suns rose in the sky at once, that light might resemble the splendor of that great being.

iast: kālo 'smi loka-kṣaya-kṛt pravṛddho lokān samāhartum iha pravṛttaḥ
en: I am Time, grown vast, destroyer of worlds, set in motion here to gather the worlds in.

deva:
सर्वधर्मान्परित्यज्य मामेकं शरणं व्रज।
अहं त्वां सर्वपापेभ्यो मोक्षयिष्यामि मा शुचः॥
iast: sarva-dharmān parityajya mām ekaṁ śaraṇaṁ vraja | ahaṁ tvāṁ sarva-pāpebhyo mokṣayiṣyāmi mā śucaḥ
en: Let go of every dharma and come to me alone for refuge. I will free you from all that stains. Do not grieve.
